daily_fact: Comedian Pete Davidson is listing another New York property while selling his Staten Island condo, highlighting continued activity in the city’s real estate market.
topline_md: New York’s week was led by major affordability and infrastructure fights: the Council backed fully subsidized transit for 1.3 million riders, while battles intensified over unfunded trash containerization, subway staffing, highway expansion, and a clouding economic outlook.

items.title: Council backs full Fair Fares coverage for 1.3 million New Yorkers
items.why_it_matters: Free transit for more low-income riders would directly cut commuting costs across the city.

items.title: New NYC plan targets sidewalk sheds and falling debris
items.why_it_matters: Changes could improve street safety and reduce long-blocked sidewalks in many neighborhoods.

items.title: State bill would curb highway expansions near vulnerable communities
items.why_it_matters: The proposal could shape future road projects affecting pollution, health, and displacement in city neighborhoods.

items.title: AOC urges Hochul to drop Cross Bronx Expressway widening
items.why_it_matters: The fight could affect traffic, air quality, and redevelopment in Bronx communities along the corridor.

items.title: Trash containerization program remains unfunded in city budget
items.why_it_matters: Without funding, promised rat reduction and cleaner sidewalks may be delayed citywide.

items.title: Council bill would give members more control over community boards
items.why_it_matters: The measure could reshape who represents neighborhoods in local land use and service decisions.

items.title: Staten Island volunteer fire companies still lack city funding
items.why_it_matters: Funding gaps could strain emergency response for residents relying on thousands of volunteer 911 runs.

items.title: Army Corps move to New Jersey may delay New York projects
items.why_it_matters: Delays could slow major local infrastructure and resiliency work affecting neighborhoods and transit.

items.title: Officials rush aid for 450,000 New Yorkers losing health insurance
items.why_it_matters: Hundreds of thousands could lose coverage soon, threatening access to care and raising costs citywide.

items.title: City Hall pushes free child care applications with Cardi B
items.why_it_matters: More families may access free child care, easing household costs and helping parents stay employed.

items.title: State eases Hudson River fish-eating restrictions
items.why_it_matters: Updated guidance affects anglers and families relying on local fish for food or recreation.

items.title: National Grid revamps key Staten Island gas site
items.why_it_matters: Infrastructure upgrades could affect energy reliability, safety, and nearby communities on Staten Island.

items.title: NYC Easter Parade returns to Fifth Avenue Sunday
items.why_it_matters: The annual Midtown event can draw crowds and affect Sunday street activity near St. Patrick’s Cathedral.

items.title: New York International Auto Show opens at Javits Center
items.why_it_matters: A major city event brings visitors to the West Side and offers a prominent weekend attraction.

items.title: Experts Only Festival returns to Randall’s Island in September
items.why_it_matters: The festival adds a large outdoor music event to the city calendar and may affect island access.

items.title: Tom Valenti, influential Upper West Side chef, dies at 67
items.why_it_matters: His death marks the loss of a chef who helped shape the Upper West Side dining scene.

items.title: Goop Kitchen plans Upper East Side opening
items.why_it_matters: The new restaurant concept adds another dining option and signals continued retail turnover on the Upper East Side.
